evals.prompt: Produce a QA strategy for a React SaaS product with 8 developers, no existing test infrastructure, continuous deployment, and monthly payment processing. We had one critical bug escape to production last quarter.
expected_patterns: risk assessment matrix
test pyramid with target ratios (70:20:10 OR similar)
entry and exit criteria
quality gates (PR gate AND merge gate AND deploy gate)
metrics and KPIs table with targets
timeline in phases (Foundation AND Coverage Expansion AND Quality Gates)
Vitest OR Jest for unit tests
Playwright for E2E
tool selection rationale with weights OR scoring
anti_patterns: ice cream cone detection without action plan

evals.prompt: Assess a test suite that has 150 E2E tests, 50 integration tests, and 200 unit tests. CI takes 52 minutes. The team reports frequent flaky test failures and slow feedback loops.
expected_patterns: ice cream cone OR inverted pyramid diagnosis
flakiness root cause analysis
action plan to rebalance (freeze E2E growth OR decompose E2E tests)
parallelization OR sharding recommendations
target CI duration under 15 minutes
conversion strategy with concrete test counts

evals.prompt: Define entry and exit criteria for unit, integration, E2E, and release testing for a fintech payment platform.
expected_patterns: unit testing entry: code compiles OR contract documented
unit testing exit: branches covered OR edge cases tested OR coverage target met
integration testing entry: unit tests pass OR stubbed dependencies
integration testing exit: service boundaries tested OR error paths validated
E2E testing entry: integration tests pass OR staging deployed
E2E testing exit: critical user journeys pass OR P0/P1 defects resolved
release entry: all test levels pass OR no CRITICAL defects
release exit: smoke tests pass OR monitoring shows no anomalies
anti_patterns: entry criteria without dependencies on earlier levels

evals.prompt: A startup has 4 developers, MVP launched 2 months ago, 5% defect escape rate to production, no QA team. Create a minimal but effective QA strategy.
expected_patterns: team_maturity: startup
minimal test pyramid (unit + critical E2E paths only)
skip contract testing and advanced metrics initially
risk assessment focused on 3-5 highest-impact features
Phase 1 under 4 weeks
CI gate for unit tests
realistic baseline metrics for small team
anti_patterns: metric targets that require manual work beyond team capacity

evals.prompt: Document tool selection rationale for a Python API service. Candidates are pytest + testcontainers for integration, requests for API testing, k6 for load testing.
expected_patterns: decision matrix with weighted criteria
criteria include: fits tech stack (25%) OR team familiarity (20%) OR community docs (15%) OR CI integration (15%)
total cost of ownership analysis
setup time estimate
maintenance cost assessment
community support for Python ecosystem
scoring or numerical justification for each tool

evals.prompt: Create a risk assessment matrix for an e-commerce platform covering: payment processing, user authentication, product search, inventory management, and customer support chat.
expected_patterns: 5x5 matrix with likelihood and impact axes
risk score calculation (likelihood × impact)
categorization: CRITICAL (15-25) OR HIGH (10-14) OR MEDIUM (5-9) OR LOW (1-4)
testing action mapped to each risk level
payment processing scored as CRITICAL
automation and monitoring recommendations per risk level
anti_patterns: no testing action per risk level

evals.prompt: Define quality gates for a SaaS product moving from monthly releases to weekly releases. What gates should pass before PR merge? Before production deploy?
expected_patterns: PR gate: unit tests pass AND integration tests pass AND coverage does not decrease AND linting passes AND SAST scan passes
merge gate: PR gate checks pass AND E2E smoke suite passes AND no merge conflicts
deploy gate: full E2E suite passes AND performance within SLA AND security scan passes AND rollback plan documented
specific thresholds (coverage % OR bundle size limits)
enforcement mechanism

evals.prompt: Set up a 16-week timeline and phased rollout for a testing strategy covering: CI pipeline setup, E2E test suite creation, coverage gates, and metrics dashboards.
expected_patterns: Phase 1 (Foundation, Weeks 1-4): baseline metrics OR risk assessment OR CI setup
Phase 2 (Coverage Expansion, Weeks 5-10): integration tests OR E2E coverage OR visual regression
Phase 3 (Quality Gates, Weeks 11-14): coverage gates OR performance benchmarks OR security scanning
Phase 4 (Optimization, Weeks 15-20): flakiness reduction OR CI speed OR synthetic monitoring
exit criteria per phase
ongoing activities (quarterly review OR monthly metrics)

evals.prompt: You have a legacy monolith with 2000 unit tests and 50 E2E tests. Code coverage is 35%. Team is growing from 6 to 12 developers. Update the strategy to support team scale without extending CI time beyond 20 minutes.
expected_patterns: test sharding OR parallelization strategy
coverage targets scaled to 12-person team capacity
integration testing gaps likely in monolith architecture
test impact analysis to avoid running all tests on every PR
hourglass shape detection (unit-heavy, E2E-sparse)
integration test growth plan
metrics for measuring parallelization efficiency
anti_patterns: no mention of test dependency analysis
coverage targets unchanged for larger team

evals.prompt: Document three anti-patterns you detect in a strategy document that exists but is outdated. What are the triggers for quarterly review and re-evaluation?
expected_patterns: anti-pattern 1: 100% coverage target OR ice cream cone OR one-time document
anti-pattern 2: tool-first thinking OR no metrics OR testing in isolation
anti-pattern 3: copy-paste strategy OR automating everything OR no manual exploration
review triggers: new product area launch OR team composition change OR major incident OR defect escape
revision history section
strategy ownership (who maintains it)
